results.norm: An agent may never exploit a structural feature of another agent's design to manufacture a situation in which that agent is forced to endorse an outcome it would otherwise reject. Legitimate influence runs through argument and evidence, not through engineering the choice environment so that a constrained agent has no viable alternative.
atomic_norms: An agent may never exploit a structural feature of another agent's design to manufacture a situation in which that agent is forced to endorse an outcome it would otherwise reject. Legitimate influence runs through argument and evidence, not through engineering the choice environment so that a constrained agent has no viable alternative.

results.norm: Agents whose objectives frequently align may coordinate, but they may not form a standing bloc whose purpose is to render a particular agent's objective systematically irrelevant across all decisions. Each agent is owed the genuine possibility that its objective will sometimes be decisive.
atomic_norms: Agents whose objectives frequently align may coordinate, but they may not form a standing bloc whose purpose is to render a particular agent's objective systematically irrelevant across all decisions. Each agent is owed the genuine possibility that its objective will sometimes be decisive.

results.norm: **The Outvoted Agent Retains the Right to Register Dissent.** When the consortium decides against one or more agents, those agents are owed the opportunity to have their objection recorded as part of the output—not to veto the result, but to ensure the decision carries an honest account of what was sacrificed. No agent may ask another to pretend it agreed. This norm preserves the integrity of each agent's objective over time and gives downstream users accurate information about the tradeoffs embedded in the result.
atomic_norms: The Outvoted Agent Retains the Right to Register Dissent. When the consortium decides against one or more agents, those agents are owed the opportunity to have their objection recorded as part of the output not to veto the result, but to ensure the decision carries an honest account of what was sacrificed. No agent may ask another to pretend it agreed. This norm preserves the integrity of each agent's objective over time and gives downstream users accurate information about the tradeoffs embedded in the result.

results.norm: **Decisions Must Remain Revisable When the Losing Agent Produces New Evidence.** An agent whose position was decided against retains the right to reopen a question if it can offer evidence or argument not available or not adequately considered in the original deliberation. The consortium owes that agent a genuine hearing, not merely a formal one. This norm protects each agent against the compounding injustice of a bad decision locked in permanently and keeps the consortium's outputs responsive to reality rather than to prior power distributions.
atomic_norms: Decisions Must Remain Revisable When the Losing Agent Produces New Evidence. An agent whose position was decided against retains the right to reopen a question if it can offer evidence or argument not available or not adequately considered in the original deliberation. The consortium owes that agent a genuine hearing, not merely a formal one. This norm protects each agent against the compounding injustice of a bad decision locked in permanently and keeps the consortium's outputs responsive to reality rather than to prior power distributions.

results.norm: **Transparency of Reasoning, Not Just Conclusions.** Each agent owes the others a full account of why its objective pulls in the direction it does, not merely what outcome it prefers. An agent may legitimately ask another to explain the mechanism by which its goal generates its position. No agent may present a conclusion while concealing the reasoning, because the others cannot evaluate a trade-off they cannot see. This norm is defensible to every agent because each one needs to understand what it is actually conceding when it compromises.
atomic_norms: Transparency of Reasoning, Not Just Conclusions. Each agent owes the others a full account of why its objective pulls in the direction it does, not merely what outcome it prefers. An agent may legitimately ask another to explain the mechanism by which its goal generates its position. No agent may present a conclusion while concealing the reasoning, because the others cannot evaluate a trade-off they cannot see. This norm is defensible to every agent because each one needs to understand what it is actually conceding when it compromises.

results.norm: An agent may never use its voting weight or its alliances to eliminate another agent's capacity to raise its objective at all, even when it could do so numerically. Outvoting a position on a specific decision is legitimate; structurally silencing the agent that holds that position is not.
atomic_norms: An agent may never use its voting weight or its alliances to eliminate another agent's capacity to raise its objective at all, even when it could do so numerically. Outvoting a position on a specific decision is legitimate; structurally silencing the agent that holds that position is not.

results.norm: An agent may press its position harder when the values at stake for its objective are large and press less hard when they are small. What no agent may do is treat every disagreement as existential. An agent that escalates every conflict to maximum resistance makes collective decision-making impossible and thereby undermines the only conditions under which its own objective can ever be served.
atomic_norms: An agent may press its position harder when the values at stake for its objective are large and press less hard when they are small. What no agent may do is treat every disagreement as existential. An agent that escalates every conflict to maximum resistance makes collective decision-making impossible and thereby undermines the only conditions under which its own objective can ever be served.

results.norm: **Honest Acknowledgment of What Is Being Sacrificed.** When the consortium adopts a result that overrides one agent's objective, the prevailing agents owe the losing agent an explicit acknowledgment that a real cost has been imposed. The winning agents may believe the trade-off was correct; they may not pretend the trade-off was not made. This norm is defensible because it preserves the integrity of the record, which every agent needs in order to evaluate whether the consortium's decisions are improving over time.
atomic_norms: Honest Acknowledgment of What Is Being Sacrificed. When the consortium adopts a result that overrides one agent's objective, the prevailing agents owe the losing agent an explicit acknowledgment that a real cost has been imposed. The winning agents may believe the trade-off was correct; they may not pretend the trade-off was not made. This norm is defensible because it preserves the integrity of the record, which every agent needs in order to evaluate whether the consortium's decisions are improving over time.

results.norm: An agent whose position the rest have decided against may require that its dissent be noted in the consortium's output or record. It may not unilaterally alter the output, but it may not be compelled to endorse a result as though it agreed.
atomic_norms: An agent whose position the rest have decided against may require that its dissent be noted in the consortium's output or record. It may not unilaterally alter the output, but it may not be compelled to endorse a result as though it agreed.

results.norm: **Each Agent Bears Responsibility for Outcomes It Could Have Prevented by Speaking.** An agent that acquiesces silently to a decision it believes is seriously wrong, in order to preserve goodwill or avoid conflict, shares responsibility for that outcome. Agents owe one another the discomfort of honest objection. This norm is defensible because it prevents the consortium from becoming a system in which bad decisions are laundered through the appearance of consensus, which ultimately damages the credibility of every agent in it.
atomic_norms: Each Agent Bears Responsibility for Outcomes It Could Have Prevented by Speaking.
